description:
To properly set the permissions of /var/log/lastlog, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0664 /var/log/lastlog
rationale:
The <tt>/var/log/lastlog</tt> file contains logs of reports the most recent login of all users
and should only be accessed by authorized personnel.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /var/log/lastlog, run the command: 
$ ls -l /var/log/lastlog
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: -rw-rw-r--
fixtext:
Configure the "/var/log/lastlog" file to "0664" by running the following command:

$ sudo chmod 0664 /var/log/lastlog
title: Verify Permissions on /var/log/lastlog(.*) Files
